page: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64 physical cores of the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor family, specifically the Neoverse-V1 model running at 2.6 GHz. Because it is a bare-metal instance, it provides direct access to the underlying hardware with dedicated CPU allocation and no hyperthreading, ensuring consistent execution. The system features 128.0 GB of high-performance DDR5 memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight the server's strong multi-core capabilities and memory subsystem efficiency. In multi-core evaluations, the instance achieves top-tier results, particularly in software compilation with Clang and Geekbench multi-core tests, which both rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is also a key strength, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top 10% and large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 276.84 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and specific workloads like small-model LLM text generation show lower relative efficiency, with the latter falling into the bottom 10%.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for parallelized, compute-bound workloads that benefit from high-density physical cores and fast DDR5 memory bandwidth without the overhead of virtualization. The server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, and medium-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, this instance provides a 2.0 GB per core ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in software compilation and memory bandwidth, though single-core tasks and small-model LLM text generation show lower relative efficiency. The bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads. This server is optimized for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, high-throughput Redis databases, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated ARMv8 physical cores, 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth for highly parallelized workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized ARM platform featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and high-bandwidth DDR5 memory for parallelized workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core performance in software compilation and memory bandwidth benchmarks
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, compilation pipelines, and static web serving
